rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH WRIGHT VS. AMANDA NOWINSKI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 31, 2019, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT AMANDA NOWINSKI's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Defendant, Cross-Defendant, and Cross-Complainant Amanda Nowinski's motion for determination of good faith settlement is denied without prejudice to its renewal upon a proper evidentiary showing. Defendant has not presented any admissible evidence that would enable the Court to determine "a rough approximation of plaintiff['s] total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v., Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) "If 'there is no substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of a settling defendant's liability, then a determination of good faith based upon such assumption is an abuse of discretion.' " (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348 [settling parties did not proffer any evidence regarding their proportionate liability for plaintiff's alleged injury, but merely presented a series of "questionable assumptions" in their memorandum to show the settlement amount was reasonable].) Counsel's declaration, which lacks personal knowledge or a proper foundation, does not constitute such substantial evidence. (Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 822, 834 [attorney's declaration regarding settling defendant's liability insufficient where he failed to provide specific supporting facts or expert opinion].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. CURTIS N CRANE MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defense Counsel'S Response To Discovery, Cooperation With Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 31, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE's Motion To Compel Defense Counsel'S Response To Discovery, Cooperation With Discovery And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Grey, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion GRANTED. Defendants are ordered to produce Dr. Crane for deposition and to produce all documents responsive to the accompanying requests for production. Defendant is ordered to appear for deposition at a mutually agreeable date in Austin, Texas (or elsewhere by agreement) before February 21 (or later by agreement). Defendant shall produce all responsive documents to the Requests for Production that have not been withdrawn at the time of the deposition. Defendant did not oppose the substance of the motion to compel document requests and his procedural arguments are without authority. Defendant waived any objection to the Notice of Deposition based upon the apparent failure to include a co-defendant on the proof of service; moreover, Defendant cites no support for his contention that failure to serve all parties with a Notice of Deposition invalidates the Notice nor excuses compliance with it. Plaintiff's Motion for Sanctions is GRANTED. Defendants are ordered to pay $1800 in sanctions for misuse of the discovery process, to wit: failing to meet and confer, failing to produce Defendant for deposition, propounding meritless objections to the Notice, including a claim for priority, and that documents pertaining to Plaintiff's claim and Defendants' medical practice were not discoverable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to grey@greylaw-sf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CARL A WESCOTT VS. KATHY LEE TROSCLAIR ESQ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 31, 2019, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT KELLI MENDEZ, KATHY TROSCLAIR's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7. Defendants Kathy Lynn Trosclair and Kelli Mendez's motion for sanctions against Plaintiff Carl A. Wescott pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 is granted. No opposition was filed. Defendants served Plaintiff with the sanctions motion by mail and email on November 27, 2018, more than 21 days before filing it, as required by the safe harbor provision of section 128.7, and Plaintiff did not withdraw his complaint. (Dees Dec. 2 & Exs 1, 2.) Defendants' declarations and the Court's docket, of which the Court takes judicial notice, establish that the allegations and claims presented in the complaint lack evidentiary support (indeed, that many of the factual allegations are false and frivolous) and/or are unlikely to have evidentiary support after a reasonable opportunity for further investigation or discovery. Further, Plaintiff has been found a vexatious litigant by this Court, has repeatedly failed to serve papers in violation of the Code of Civil Procedure, the Rules of Court, and the Court's orders, and has been the subject of several sanctions awards by this Court. The only reasonable inference is that the complaint was presented primarily for an improper purpose, such as to harass or to cause unnecessary delay. Accordingly, the Court strikes Plaintiff's complaint and dismisses this action with prejudice, and awards Defendants $5,000 in monetary sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order stating that the motion was unopposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ALESSANDRO GARCIA VS. MALIN + GOETZ SKINCARE INC. A NEW YORK CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Subpoenas To Saks Fifth Avenue Custodian Of Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 31, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTS ROBERT WILLIAMS, ROBERT FLANAGAN, and BLOOMINGDALE'S INC.'s Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Subpoenas To Saks Fifth Avenue Custodian Of Records Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Grey, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion to Quash is GRANTED in part, and DENIED in part. Defendants' Motion is GRANTED in full as to Marsha Sanders, as she is not the subject of a negligent hiring claim, nor was there was a sufficient factual showing of the nexus between her former employment and the instant matter. It is further GRANTED with respect to Document Request Nos: 1, 2, 5, 6, 9, 10, 11, 12 as to Defendants Williams and Flanagan. It is DENIED to those remaining document requests which pertain to previous instances, if any, of wrongful conduct which are directly relevant to the instant disputes, including to the negligent hiring and retention claims. While Defendants are correct that discovery into private personnel documents should be pursued in the least intrusive manner, this does not mean Plaintiff is limited to the testimony of the defendants and witness(es) without the opportunity to test or refute that testimony. While previous instances of misconduct would be relevant to Plaintiff's claims, there is no showing that the entire personnel file is discoverable here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to grey@greylaw-sf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALESSANDRO GARCIA VS. MALIN + GOETZ SKINCARE INC. A NEW YORK CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 31, 2019, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT ROBERT WILLIAMS, ROBERT FLANAGAN, BLOOMINGDALE'S INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants Robert Williams, Robert W. Flanagan, and Bloomingdale's Inc.'s motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued per Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(h). Plaintiff has made a good faith showing by affidavit that facts essential to justify opposition to the motion may exist but cannot be presented absent a continuance. In particular, Plaintiff's pending discovery requests and related motions to compel seek to obtain evidence bearing directly on Defendants' motion, which turns in large part on such issues as whether Bloomingdale's exercised sufficient control over Plaintiff's employment to be considered his employer for purposes of FEHA and whether Plaintiff or Malin + Goetz was a "special employee" of Bloomingdale's or "a person providing services pursuant to a contract" within the meaning of Government Code section 12940(j)(5). (See, e.g., Hirst v. City of Oceanside (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 774, 785-786; McCoy v. Pacific Maritime Ass'n (2015) 216 Cal.App.4th 283, 301-303.) Plaintiff has shown that additional time is required to complete discovery due to the recent death of defendant Robert Williams and to Defendants' refusal to provide complete responses to outstanding discovery requests. Even if Plaintiff could have shown more diligence in initiating discovery, the strong public policy favoring disposition of cases on their merits warrants a continuance of the hearing. (See Chavez v. 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc. (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 632, 643-644; Bahl v. Bank of America (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 389, 397-400.) The parties need not appear on January 31 in Department 206 on Plaintiff's motion to continue the current March 4, 2019 trial date. The Court intends to vacate and continue that trial date. The parties should be prepared at the hearing to propose a reasonable schedule for completion of discovery, including the need to meet and confer regarding further responses, the dates for supplemental briefing and the continued hearing on Defendants' motion, and the new trial date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGIANA SPRADLING ET AL VS. LEEANN BORAGNO LEEPER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Justin Goodman And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 31, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT LEEANN LEEPER's Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Justin Goodman And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Grey, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion to Quash is GRANTED. Attorney Goodman's participation in the transactions regarding the pre-litigation landlord tenant dispute does render him a witness to those events. His communications with third parties is not protected. Plaintiff contends that the decisions about the use or non-use of the EnviroNova and other mold results are important to her claims. However, that area of inquiry necessarily entails Goodman's "impressions, conclusions, opinions, or legal research or theories." After prohibiting discovery into privileged areas of inquiry including attorney-client communication, impressions, conclusions and opinions (including those regarding the use or non-use of test results), and writings reflecting either, there is little to no utility in the deposition. Thus, there is not "extremely good cause" to overcome the presumption that depositions of opposing counsel are improper. Cross Motions for Sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to grey@greylaw-sf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
